title: AMD Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Advanced Micro Devices delivered exceptional Q1 2026 results, with revenue of $10.3 billion exceeding the $9.85 billion forecast. The company reported earnings per share of $1.37, surpassing analyst expectations of $1.27. Stock prices rose 4.02% in aftermarket trading following the announcement. Revenue grew 38% year-over-year. EPS increased 43% year-over-year. Free cash flow reached $2.6 billion, more than tripling year-over-year. Gross margin was 55%, up 170 basis points. Data Center segment grew 57% year-over-year to $5.8 billion. The Data Center segment emerged as the primary growth driver. CEO Lisa Su noted: 'Growth was broad-based, with every segment increasing year-over-year, led by 57% data center revenue growth.' Client and Gaming revenue reached $3.6 billion (up 23%), while Embedded grew 6% to $873 million. AMD projects Q2 2026 revenue of approximately $11.2 billion, representing 46% year-over-year growth. The company anticipates gross margins around 56% and non-GAAP operating expenses of $3.3 billion. Management significantly revised server CPU market projections, projecting the TAM will exceed $120 billion by 2030, growing at over 35% annually—substantially higher than previous 18% estimates. This reflects accelerating demand for CPU compute supporting AI infrastructure deployments.
